content.full_text:
RAVI MEHTA
CHAIRMAN

FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION

September 3, 1997


David M. Slosberg
Political Campaign Reporting
1818 Gilbreth Road, Suite 149
Burlingame, California 94010

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-97-435

Dear Mr. Slosberg:

This letter is in response to your request for advice regarding the provisions of the
Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹

QUESTION AND CONCLUSION

In a jurisdiction which has not adopted voluntary spending limits, what is the maximum
contribution allowed from a political party to a local candidate?

We have advised that candidates in local jurisdictions that have not adopted voluntary
expenditure ceilings, the limits of Section 85304 (which applies to limitations on contributions
from political parties) are 25 percent of the recommended spending ceiling in Section 85400(e),
which is $1 per resident per election in the district in which the candidate is seeking elective
office. (Moll Advice Letter, I-97-0133; Ramirez Advice Letter, No. A-97-262, copies enclosed).
For candidates in local jurisdictions that have expenditure ceilings, the limit on contributions
from political parties is 25 percent of that ceiling.


___________________________
¹ Government Code sections 81000 - 91014. Commission regulations appear at title 2, sections 18109 -
18995, of the California Code of Regulations.


428 J STREET, SUITE 800, SACRAMENTO, CALIFORNIA 95814 (916) 322-5660

File No. A-97-435
Page 2

If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-
5660.

Sincerely,

Steven G. Churchwell
General Counsel

By: Jill Stecher
Staff Counsel, Legal Division

SGC:JS:jlw

Enclosures

I:\LEGALDIV\Advice Ltrs\97435.wpd
